summary: Range: Only use the snapped value if it is different enough from the entered value

description.text: Fixes #109838 (not a regression fix this time, this PR is actually an improvement over what Godot has ever had before) @jan-kapoli commented (but deleted their comment?) asking why we are snapping at all. Well, the reason why is because this is the behavior of Range. However, that still raises a good point. If the value is already good, we shouldn't need to snap it. This PR checks if the value is approximately equal to the snapped value, and uses the original value. This now works correctly even for values and step sizes down to `1e-7`: Snapping will still occur if the value is different enough. For example, with a step size of `0.0000001`, if you enter `0.000000101`, this gets converted to `9.999999999994822e-08` instead of `0.0000001`, but `0.0000001` gets preserved as the closest possible float to `0.0000001` (and therefore serialized as `1e-7`). The behavior in this PR may be slightly worse if you don't trust the user's input, allowing the value to be up to `1e-14` away from the snapped value rather than force-snapping to within `1e-15` or `1e-16` of the best value. However, I think this is a very minor problem (only slightly worse than the imprecision `double` already provides), and has the benefit that if you do trust the user's input and expect that the value the user entered is *probably* already good, this helps.

summary: Focus events when embedding game in editor behaves differently on macOS

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.beta7

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta7 - macOS Sequoia (15.5.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Pro (Apple8) - Apple M2 Pro (10 threads) - 32.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

This is an off-shoot from #109633, which was partially addressed in #109724.

On Windows and Linux (X11) the focus events are emitted as follows:

- When in either mode, both application and window enter events are emitted when the user first hovers over the game viewport.
- When in either mode, both application and window exit events are emitted when the user clicks outside of the game viewport.
- When in floating mode, both application and window exit events are emitted when focus of the floating workspace window is lost.

However, on macOS, it now behaves as follows after #109724 was merged:

- When in either mode, only window focus events are emitted based on mouse input within the editor, and only when clicking, not when hovering[^1].
- When in floating mode, window exit events are emitted when focus of the floating workspace window is lost.
- When in non-floating mode, losing focus of the editor window will emit both application and window exit events.
- When in non-floating mode, gaining focus of the editor window will emit both application and window enter events.

It's unclear what the correct behavior should be in this case, and I would argue both have some weird quirks, but the fact that the behavior is different between different desktop platforms would seem to be somewhat problematic.

[^1]: `InputEventMouseMotion` is still sent to the application when unfocused though.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP on macOS.
2. Try both the floating and non-floating embedded mode.
3. Open the MRP on Windows or Linux/X11.
4. Try both the floating and non-floating embedded mode.
5. Note the difference in behavior.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[focus-events.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22082393/focus-events.zip)

summary: `OS.get_processor_name`doesn't seem to work on iOS

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.beta7

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta7 - iPhone XR - iOS 18.3.1

### Issue description

Put simply, calling `OS.get_processor_name` on iOS will seemingly always result in an error like so:

```
drivers/apple_embedded/os_apple_embedded.mm:419:get_processor_name(): Couldn't get the CPU model name. Returning an empty string. Method/function failed. Returning: ""
```

This seems to be due to `OS_AppleEmbedded::get_processor_name` relying on the `sysctl` name `machdep.cpu.brand_string`, which I believe only applies to macOS, but I could be wrong.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/52a5644b5f85eed80016c2dff905804e869df144/drivers/apple_embedded/os_apple_embedded.mm#L413-L420

I haven't tested this with older versions of Godot, but seeing as how this code hasn't changed in a while I have no reason to believe this is a recent regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP project.
2. Export MRP project to iOS.
3. Run the project on an iOS device.
4. Note the failure/error.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[ios-processor-name.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22081806/ios-processor-name.zip)

summary: "Create Collision Shape" operates on old version of a mesh when asset updates

description.text:
### Tested versions

 - Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7640) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9900K CPU @ 3.60GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

Godot seems to operate on a cached version of the mesh data when Creating Collision Shapes from it, despite the mesh updating visually.

### Steps to reproduce

_This is done with a `.blend` automatic import_

1. Add a `.blend` to a scene
2. Right click on the new node and toggle "Editable Children" to see the mesh
    a. Optionally, you can create a New Inherited Scene and observe the same results
3. Select the child mesh
4. Open the Mesh menu and select "Create Collision Shape..."
5. Hit "Create" and observe that your `CollisionShape3D` is created
6. Open the file in Blender
7. Edit the mesh geometry & save
8. Return to Godot
9. Observe that all of your asset re-imports and all changes are visible in the scene as expected
10. Repeat steps 1 through 5 above
11. Observe that the new `CollisionShape3D` matches the **old** geometry, not matching your edits

I thought this might be intended (or some some other feature of an Inherited Scene that I'm not familiar with), but it  stops happening if you close and reopen Godot, so it seems like something is cached in-memory and isn't being updated the way the rest of the attributes get re-imported.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: ColorPicker UI improvements after adding intensity slider.

description.text:
**changes:**

- Sorted intensity slider to appear after the alpha slider for consistency with other slider styles.

- Added icon for hex code input, matching the new script code icon style.

- Modes are now [RGB, HSV, Linear, OKHSL], displayed horizontally without an extra popup for OKHSL.

- Moved colorize sliders option to theme constants (purely visual change) and removed the mode `PopupMenu`.

- Added editor setting: `color_picker_colorize_sliders`.

- Changed the `label_width` from 10 to 16 to prevent the sliders from moving when changing modes.

- Changed the sampler to use `Control` node instead of `TextureRect` since it 's not using the `texture` property.

- Removed the `Hex/Expr` Label, maybe we can provide a better tooltip text for the hex button instead.

summary: IME preedit apostrophe (U+0027) leaks into committed text → multiple regressions in editor since 4.3/4.4 (TextEdit caret error; Find-in-Files desync; apostrophes between letters)

description.text:
# Tested Versions

###  Observed issues

**① Script Editor (TextEdit)**  
---

Chinese IME composition can be interrupted (✅ expected),  
but after interruption, the **pinyin syllable separator `'` (U+0027)** is mistakenly committed as normal text.  
e.g. typing *fenhao* produces `fen'hao`.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a1d73159-e0aa-4a26-8ca3-1db6e1c45fab


**② “Find in Files” search field**  
---

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8d9a3019-d28c-48d4-a92a-2b16340a818d

After IME interruption,  `'` remains.  
After interruption, visible Latin letters **aren’t recognized by search** until the user edits once.



**③ Error flood below "#" (later confirmed to be hover-related)**  
---

Below the "#", Typing Chinese in a script comment and interrupting it leaves pinyin and triggers **a flood of errors** (especially in 4.5.dev5).  
Later investigation confirmed this was **actually caused by IME text retrieval during mouse hover**,  
and it was fully fixed by [PR #111859](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/111859).  
✅ **Case③ fixed.**

**③-b Hover flood after parser error (follow-up observation)
---

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/07773610-0e4f-4015-8483-258c75f83a9e

After a Parser Error, hovering the mouse over the affected line often triggers a flood of errors:

`ERROR: scene\gui\code_edit.cpp:2035 - Condition "p_column - 1 > get_line(p_line).size()" is true. Returning: Point2(-1, -1)`

See the reproduction steps below.

Steps to Reproduce case③-b:
---
STEP1: Open Godot and create a new GDScript file.

STEP2: Type with the Chinese IME, interrupt the input to produce composed text containing apostrophes (for example: w'w'w'w).

STEP3: Wait for the usual minor errors to appear (these are not the focus here).

STEP4: Repeat step ② two or three times to make the bug more likely to trigger.

STEP5: Hover the mouse over the affected line.

---

 Reproducible in
---
| Version | Cases |
|----------|--------|
| 4.3 | ① |
| 4.4 / 4.4.1–4.5.dev4 | ① + ② |
| 4.5.dev5 | ① + ② + ③+③b (error flood) |
| 4.5.beta1–4.6.dev2 | ① + ② +③b(③ fixed by #111859) |
|4.6.dev(After #111859 merged)|①+②+③b|

**Not reproducible in:**  
4.0.stable–4.2.2 (and 3.x)

---

# System Information

- **OS:** Windows 10 / Windows 11  
- **IME:** Microsoft Pinyin / Sogou Pinyin

---

# Summary

Since **Godot 4.3**, the editor correctly allows **interrupting IME preedit** (✅ expected).  
However, when composition ends (usually via **mouse click** or **focus change**),  
the **pinyin separator `'`** is mistakenly treated as committed text.

---

| Area | Symptom | Note |
|------|----------|------|
| **Script Editor (TextEdit)** | Apostrophes `'` remain between Latin letters (`ni'hao`), caret position may desync. | Since 4.3 |
| **Find in Files** | Latin letters not recognized until edit; `'` inserted unexpectedly. | Since 4.4 |
| **Case③: Error flood** | Later confirmed hover-related, fixed by [#111859](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/111859). | 4.5.dev5 |

---

#  Root Cause (Technical)

Windows IME sends `WM_IME_COMPOSITION` with `GCS_RESULTSTR` when ending composition.  
Godot currently enqueues these characters (including `'`) into `_process_key_events()`,  
so `'` (U+0027) enters text as valid input, breaking caret and validation logic.

When `'` is filtered out during `ENDCOMPOSITION` and caret position is preserved,  
**Case① and Case② both disappear** (verified).

---

# ✅ Expected

- Apostrophe `'` should **not** appear in committed text.  

e.g
---

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/71f0ec29-ffe8-435a-a1f3-947d0f53e2e8

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f0863a43-2a66-479e-831b-c763bd483640

- TextEdit & Find in Files should **revalidate immediately** after IME ends.  
- No error flood when switching focus or hovering (Case③ fixed).

---

# ❌ Actual

- `'` remains (`ni'hao`).  
- Find in Files doesn’t recognize visible Latin letters.  
- (Old) Error flood on hover — fixed by [#111859](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/111859).

---

#  Notes

> Case② depends on Case① — once `'` stops leaking, Find in Files works normally.  
> Both belong to the same root cause (apostrophe leak at IME end).  
> Case③ turned out to be hover-related and was fixed by [#111859](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/111859).

---

 **In short:**  
Case① is the root cause; Case② is its chain reaction;  
Case③ looked related but was actually hover-handling, now fixed separately.
and Case③-b remains. (by the way,I test #100787  ,it seems  to fix both Case③ and Case③-b )

summary: Improve deselection in AnimationTree editors and inspector

description.text:
This PR seeks to make selection in the `AnimationTree`'s editors more consistent with how it is in `SceneTree`, in service of future improvements. At the moment, when a node is deselected, either by erasing it or clicking in empty space, neither the inspector nor the erase tool reflect this change.

In this commit,
- For `BlendSpace1D`, `BlendSpace2D` and `StateMachine`, when an empty area is clicked or a selection is deleted, the inspector's selection reverts to the host node that encompasses the current editor.
- For `BlendTree`, in addition to the above, selections are now retained when the graph is redrawn, caused by events such as connecting nodes or deleting nodes by pressing their top-right cross.

summary: Crash when synthetic and native mouse clicks are sent over a visible Control

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6]
v4.5.beta7.official [4ebf67c12]

also tested with gdscript and it crashes as well

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Fedora Linux 42 (Adams) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650 SUPER (nvidia; 575.64.05) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4790K CPU @ 4.00GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Game crashes with no error message when certain rapid synthetic mouse events (via Input.ParseInputEvent) are sent while native mouse clicks occur over a visible Control.

Specifically, it is reliably reproduced when quickly clicking left and right mouse buttons in the MRP *inside* the area of the control.

The crash does not occur if the Control is invisible, and it only triggers when both synthetic and native clicks interact with the Control.
You can work around the crash by adding metadata to your synthetic events and doing something like ```if (!ev.HasMeta("virtual_mouse")) GetViewport().SetInputAsHandled();```. 

### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a duplicate of mouse event. Add metadata such as "virtual_mouse" to prevent infinite recursion.
2. Add a control to the scene. Resize it to fill some of the screen.
3. Rapidly click the left and right mouse buttons within the Control area.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[synethtic-click-crash-mrp-gdscript.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22061531/synethtic-click-crash-mrp-gdscript.zip)

summary: Remove arbitrary limits on BlendSpace editors' visible region

description.text:
BlendSpace1D and BlendSpace2D's editors do not allow the min value of their space to be greater than 0, and the max value to be less than 0.01. However, `animation_blend_space_1d.cpp` and `animation_blend_space_2d.cpp` only enforce that the min limit be less than the max limit.

Considering the user's ability to change `value` in BlendSpace1D, and `x` and `y` in BlendSpace2D to anything, one assumes that they should be able to map these BlendSpaces' visible area 1:1 to the range of the specific variable that controls them in their code, even if that range is all negative.

Safeguarding the visibility of 0 so that the user doesn't get disoriented seems to me to be the only reason for this, but that can be rectified by a future PR that adds numbering to grid divisions as well.

Additionally, grid lines are never drawn when at the max edge, that is also fixed here.

summary: SubViewport scaling settings copy project settings on initialization if not overriden

description.text:
### Tested versions

Since the relevant lines were introduced in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/51870/files#diff-7d37141bff1285fa01a0530677bdd0e2d07f1a3d6a9644b7eb5555807b8445c0R3758 this issue should exist in all versions since v4.0.0dev and can be reproduced until at least https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/4ebf67c12dcdffcb69242569c118a371a654b6ae (from a few days ago) which (IIRC) was the commit I used to test the fix.

### System information

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] (version on which the bug was discovered)

### Issue description

When a SubViewport is created in the editor, through code or a project with a SubViewport is reloaded (or opened), any setting under "Scaling 3D" (the editor grouping is incidental but matches all affected properties), if not overriden already, will be set to the same value from project settings and now that new value will become its default.

Related to #105509 (for which there is already a fix in #110005 likely to be superceded by a simpler one). In that issue, we encountered a problem with SubViewport and FSR2 because our project settings were set to FSR2 and, in my case there was a small SubViewport in the scene, in the case of the OP they made a SubViewport that was small through code. The FSR2 issue is resolved by the linked PR. This report is about the implicit setting of SubViewport properties to match project settings, which seems to be a bug based on discussion in godotengine/godot-proposals#13084.

### Steps to reproduce

This behvior occurs whenever a setting under a SubViewport's "Scaling 3D" section is not overriden and the constructor is called, which is when we make a SubViewport through script, make a new one in the editor or load a projec that contains a SubViewport in a scene.

Reproducing just requires setting a relevant property in the project settings, _not_ overriding the property in the SubViewport, triggering the constructor through one of the above ways and checking what the settings in the SubViewport are.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[subviewport-following-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22058772/subviewport-following-mrp.zip)

This is easy to reproduce even without an MRP but for convenience and reference, this is the one I used to diagnose the issue.

summary: Duplicate 3D nodes when pressing shift during transform

description.text:
Fixes godotengine/godot-proposals#1381

I decided to implement this for `shift` instead of `alt`. The reasoning is that there would be too many conflicts otherwise. Most importantly, `alt` is being used when dragging across the viewport: just left click does a box select, `alt` left click uses the currently active transform tool. So we can not use alt for object duplication, leaving us with `shift` as the modifier for this feature.

The general logic in this PR is:
- `_compute_edit()` is called at the start of all transforms, so that is where we duplicate the nodes before the transform
- `_edit` state data keeps track of the duplication
- `cancel_transform()` removes the duplicated nodes to restore the state before the transform was started
- `commit_transform()` commits the duplicated nodes to history

The object duplication mostly follows the same logic as the normal duplication in the scene tree with a few differences:
- We don't immediately commit the action to undo/redo, instead we keep track of the changes in our `EditData` state object. This allows us to do a rollback in case the transform is cancelled without effecting history. And when a transform is committed the we simply add the duplication to history bundled together with the transform. This leads to a clean history.
- In the context of transforming, it doesn't make sense to abort completely for an illegal operation like duplicating the root node. Instead, we still go on with the transform. We just skip the duplication and show a warning as a toast. This should be more user-friendly than just printing an error and doing nothing.

This PR only implements duplication for spatial gizmos of nodes, it does not make sense for any other kinds of gizmos (like the handles of a physics shape for example).

summary: Fix JSON modification out of sync

description.text:
A json file can be modified in three places: the inspector, the internal editor, and the external editor.

Make sure that modifying the JSON in one place is synchronized to the other two.

Fix #104943, supersede #105134.

Note: Opening a json file in the internal editor will automatically reload from the file. This will discard any unsaved changes in the Inspector. To restore these unsaved changes, **undo and then redo** them in the Inspector.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.5-beta6_mono_winx64_console

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta6.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7680) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900K (32 threads) - 127.7 GiB memory

### Issue description

I am integrating Godot into my build system which uses CMake to orchestrate everything. I have a custom command to invoke godot to export like so:
```bash
Godot_v4.5-beta6_mono_win64_console.exe --headless --path D:/Path/ToProject --export-release "Windows X64" D:/Path/To/ExportedProject/Project.exe
```

However, in some cases it will hang forever. I have narrowed it down to the fact that the console executable will create a job object which ensures it waits for not just Godot_v4.5-beta6_mono_win64.exe but all child processes too. And the child process that is causing this is dotnet. It will spawn its own child process that acts as a server, something like

```
"C:\Program Files\dotnet\dotnet.EXE" exec "C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk\8.0.413\Roslyn\bincore\VBCSCompiler.dll" "-pipename:gVonHoCttKisj5gIrT8zv4dO0uypzqF93B2HRjR7Lvg"
```

This server sticks around for a long time, past the lifetime of the parent dotnet process which started it. This keeps the godot console waiting on the job object.

On subsequent attempts this will not reproduce if the dotnet server is already running but I can consistently reproduce it by ensuring all "dotnet" processes have been terminated first and deleting the .godot folder so that C# code is recompiled again.

There are four possible fixes or workaround that I have found:
* Setting the "UseSharedCompilation" environment variable to "false".
* Setting `<UseSharedCompilation>false</UseSharedCompilation>` inside the .csproj file.
* Writing a proxy script to invoke Godot_v4.5-beta6_mono_win64.exe (non-console version) and explicitly waiting on it to complete. This will avoid the job object which seems to not be necessary (at least in this case anyway).
* Using `dotnet build-server shutdown --vbcscompiler` to stop the build server. I believe this might be the best approach for Godot to run after export is complete. This one is not so much a workaround, but a potential fix Godot might want to use?

Presumably setting `UseSharedCompilation` to false will have some negative impact on dotnet compile times.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create new Godot project
2. Add a node
3. Add a C# script to the node. Leave it default
4. Add a Windows Desktop export preset. Use defaults.
5. Run export from command-line ensuring .godot has been deleted first and there are no dotnet.exe processes running
6. It should hang forever. If you terminate all dotnet.exe processes, it will complete successfully.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[dotnet-hang-repro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22054716/dotnet-hang-repro.zip)

My export command:
```bash
.\Godot_v4.5-beta6_mono_win64_console.exe --headless --path C:\Users\bryceh\Documents\dotnet-hang-repro\ --export-release "Windows Desktop" C:\temp\HangRepro.exe
```

summary: Memory leak when resetting PackedByteArray size in Inspector

description.text:
### Tested versions


- Reproducible in: 4.4.1-stable, 4.1-stable
- Not reproducible in: 4.0-stable

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

When using an exported PackedByteArray in the editor, resetting its Size in the Inspector does not free the memory previously allocated.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Attach a new script to any Node.
2. In the script, write the following code:
```GDScript
extends Node

@export var array: PackedByteArray
```
3. Select the Node with the attached script in the editor.
4. In the Inspector, click on the array property.
5. Set the Size field to 134217728 (128 MiB).
6. Then, set the Size field back to 0.
7. Observe that Godot does not free the 128 MiB of memory that was previously allocated.

summary: [4.3 - 4.5] DirectX12 AMD Radeon Broken When Using Enhanced Barriers

description.text: ### Tested versions 4.5dev [d5ad0556a2c04c50694b5c04dc1b3bf03ecd7113] 4.3dev ### System information Windows 10 4.5 D3D12 12_0 - Forward Mobile AMD Radeon RX 6500 XT ### Issue description We had plenty of people with AMD Radeon GPUs telling us that rendering on their PC looked broken. After acquiring an AMD Radeon of our own we could reproduce this issue: Further debugging and comparing of RenderDoc traces brought to light: Enabling Enhanced Barriers in the rendering pipeline leads to ResolveSubresource calls without the inserting the needed barriers beforehand. Other calls are probably also faulty, but for this one I could verify it by manually adding the barriers in a C++ dump from pix. That leads to random state, in places where one would not suspect issues. In our case it mostly fails for shaders whose output depend on the screen texture. **Correct** (Working) RenderDoc Trace: **Incorrect** (Non-Working) RenderDoc Trace: One can find the respective RenderDoc trace files here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Ha1EATLuHDND0ba8ulvkwvtAmBWaxCVz?usp=sharing these traces where made with a few days old master branch. We fixed this now for our branch by disabling the barriers. This is especially bad since the only working backend on AMD then is Angle/OpenGL. ### Steps to reproduce Start any Godot game using sub viewports using MSAA and rendering the associated viewport texture. Notice that the textures go into a corrupted state when running the game on a somewhat recent AMD GPU. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) TODO

summary: CollisionObject2D's `input_event` signal emits inconsistently when subwindows are created

description.text:
### Tested versions

-Reproducible on 4.5beta6, 4.4.1, 4.3

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 7 2700X Eight-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

i would like to make a game that makes heavy use of subwindows, while retaining input from the underlying game. i use Area2D's `input_event` signal to process mouse inputs on certain regions of a world map, and summon windows for more information when necessary. unfortunately, it seems like as soon as a window is summoned, pretty much all input is consumed by the subwindow. However, there are some strange caveats:

- input registers as soon as the mouse enters/exits an area
- input registers when clicking within an area
- input registers just fine on the area, *assuming* the window's titlebar is on top of it.

i suspect that my setup probably has some incorrectly tweaked options to allow for continuous input on the underlying areas in addition to input on the window (any guidance on that would be appreciated), but even beyond that, the above behavior suggests that something isn't working properly. i've attached a video to show what i mean:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/37b10546-49ca-43f3-a69c-ad87d3274f45

here, you can see that the input processes normally as before as long as i keep the cursor within the confines of the window titlebar and hovered over the area.

edit: also i can set `unfocusable` to `true` on the window to cause the area to behave normally, but then i lose input on the window (the LineEdit was added to test this)

### Steps to reproduce

- hook up the `input_event` signal to an Area2D
- add a Window node to the scene somehow
- test input on the area with and without the window overlapping

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[Window Input Passing.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22049127/Window.Input.Passing.zip)

summary: Forward Mobile: When lights have distant faders set, close lights can be disabled due to max lights hard limit.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3.1.stable

### System information

Windows 10 - Forward Mobile

### Issue description

When more than 8 omnilights or spotlights do affect a single mesh in Forward Mobile renderer and all the lights have distant faders set up, the first 8 lights to be enabled by the faders will be the one that will get sent to the mesh. This is a problem when distant faders are used to selectively disable distant lights in favor of enabling close ones since the close ones can eventually be nots ent to the mesh.

A possible solution would be to sort lights by distance (the very distance the distant faders use to calculate when to enable/disable lights) prior sending them as candidates for a mesh draw.

Here's a small video showcasing the issue:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5324d0cf-709e-478a-875b-e03e5e843a1f

### Steps to reproduce

In order to reproduce the issue with the sample project, just move around the camera like in the video.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[lights.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22044861/lights.zip)

summary: Add `Viewport` mipmap generation support

description.text:
Adds an option to the viewport to generate mipmaps after rendering. This is useful with a SubViewport when using the ViewportTexture as a texture in 3d, since mipmaps can be utilized.

The gdscript member `use_mipmaps` is added to the Viewport class. The gdscript method `viewport_set_use_mipmaps` is also added to the RenderingServer. The feature can be used by adding a SubViewport  and toggling on Use Mipmaps.

The option is turned off by default. All rendering functionality is identical to before if the option is left off.

Related:
godotengine/godot-proposals#7576
#23417 (although it specifies 3.x)

* *Bugsquad edit, closes: godotengine/godot-proposals#7576*

summary: Godot crashes on exit due to betsy s3tc compression

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4.1, 4.5.beta6

Calling `Image.compress(Image.COMPRESS_S3TC)` or importing textures with S3TC compression causes Godot to crash on exit:

```gdscript
@tool
extends Node

func _ready() -> void:

	img.compress(Image.COMPRESS_S3TC)
```

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8088) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

Getting a warning after an addon is deleted (in Godot FileSystem):
``
  WARNING: editor/editor_node.cpp:3687 - Addon 'res://addons/myplugin/plugin.cfg' failed to load. No directory found. Removing from enabled plugins.
``
The issue is that the `project.godot `file still contain this line:
``
enabled=PackedStringArray("res://addons/myplugin/plugin.cfg")
``
In the Project Settings -> Plugins tab the list is empty.
The warning only disappears once the line is manually removed from `project.godot`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Project Settings -> Plugins -> Create New Plugin
2. Name it -> Create
3. File System -> Select addon and delete from there.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[minimal-reproduction-project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22019317/minimal-reproduction-project.zip)

summary: Validate theme type name input in Add Theme Type dialog

description.text:
Closes #104297

There are four places where users can provide the name of a theme type:

1. Clicking "+" button in the Theme bottom panel to add a new theme type.
2. Clicking "I" button in the Theme bottom panel to rename an existing theme type.
3. Clicking "+" button in the Manage Theme Items dialog to add a new theme type.
4. Double clicking an theme type list item in the Manage Theme Items dialog to rename an existing theme type.

Currently, place 1 does no check while the others mistakenly prohibit the use of names beginning with numbers.

This PR fixes the validation and adds the missing check in place 1.

p.s. Also optimized `Theme::is_valid_type_name()` and `Theme::is_valid_item_name()`.

summary: Use symbols instead of text when possible to shorten PopupMenu shortcut labels

description.text:
Implements Proposal [#13066](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13066). Any Unicode keys that are available to be used as shortcuts now are displayed as the symbol literal on PopupMenu children of a MenuBar with shortcuts of that key.

For testing, I created a [simple project](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22014691/shortcuts-test.zip) to display all the possible shortcuts.

For keypad-specific symbols, I chose to match the style of the keypad numbers and label them as "Kp <symbol>" so that they are distinct from the normal symbol key. Changing that, or adding/removing any symbols would be as simple as modifying `_shortcut_symbols`.

summary: Creating a shader in a scene that hasn't been saved yet defaults to an invalid UID-like path

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.5.beta6, 4.5.beta 4ebf67c12 - Not reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable ### System information Godot v4.5.beta (4ebf67c12) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7688) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 63.55 GiB memory ### Issue description When you create a new shader using the shader creation dialog in the **New Shader** resource dropdown, the file name will be set to an invalid UID-based filename: This file name is illegal on Windows due to `:`, so the shader can't be created: This only occurs if you're creating the shader in a scene that hasn't been saved yet. If the scene has been saved, then the shader will be given a valid `res://` path by default. In 4.4.1.stable, an empty `res://` path was provided instead, which is still invalid, but at least it was consistently invalid on all platforms (and not looking random): ### Steps to reproduce - Create a Node3D and a MeshInstance3D child. Add a new BoxMesh to the MeshInstance3D child. *Do not save the scene at this point.* - Add a new ShaderMaterial to the BoxMesh resource, then a new Shader in the ShaderMaterial resource. - In the dialog that appears, notice how the file name starts with `::` and looks like an UID. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in 4.3, 4.4.1, 4.5 beta 6 ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads) ### Issue description `snap_2d_vertices_to_pixel` setting for viewports causes `Line2D` nodes to render round endcaps with pixel-sized holes between the cap and the actual line.
